faqs.a: Akathisia is an intense inner restlessness paired with a hard-to-resist urge to move, often described as not being able to sit still or a buzzing under the skin. From the inside it tends to feel worse than it looks from the outside.

faqs.q: Is akathisia the same as anxiety?
faqs.a: No. Although it can feel similar, the key clue with akathisia is a physical, motor-driven urge to move and its timing right after a medication is started or a dose is raised. Treating it differs from treating an anxiety flare.

faqs.q: What should I do if I think I have akathisia?
faqs.a: Akathisia can be serious and distressing, so it deserves prompt contact with a prescriber rather than pushing through it or stopping the medication on your own. Reporting it early gives the best chance of quick relief.
